mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate discount rates and lower borrowing costs, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a UNRATE rise above the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures and forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth rates exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong corporate earnings momentum, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the mechanism predicts positive returns but actual returns are negative in >50% of cases when INDPRO exceeds its trend.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which boosts related equity sectors and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (50%) in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating beyond a 0.5% monthly threshold forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, raising real discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly growth above 0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding potential output signals an overheating economy with strong corporate earnings, which generates immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 outperformance fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early expansions, or the signal reverses to negative predictive power when real GDP growth surpasses 4% annualized.
